R5.verification: Grader confirms either the re-sorted QA-filtered Trending top with filter persistence, or the recorded current global/homepage Trending dataset.

query: I want a free, popular classic to start reading. On Project Gutenberg, find a popular English-language book and give me its title, author, release date, and a couple of formats I can download (like EPUB or plain text). Then add one fact about it - genre or first publication year - from Wikipedia.

R1.requirement: On Project Gutenberg, reach a popularity-ranked list of books by any of these paths: a search/browse with 'Sort By: Popularity' (URL sort_order=downloads), the 'Frequently Downloaded' section, or the Top 100 downloads list; record which path you used and the evidence (the sort control/URL, or the Frequently Downloaded / Top 100 heading).
R1.verification: Grader confirms a popularity-ranked Gutenberg list via any of the accepted paths (Sort By: Popularity / sort_order=downloads, Frequently Downloaded, or Top 100) from the recorded evidence.

R2.requirement: Open the top listed book and record title, author, release date, and download count if shown.
R2.verification: Grader opens the book page and matches the fields.

R3.requirement: Record at least two available formats (e.g. EPUB, Plain Text) and the link type.
R3.verification: Grader confirms the two formats on the book page.

R4.requirement: Open the English Wikipedia article for the book or author and record one context fact (genre or first publication year).
R4.verification: Grader opens the Wikipedia page and matches the context.

query: I coordinate a small geohazards desk and I need to brief my team on the most significant recent global earthquakes, so I want to work through the USGS Latest Earthquakes application. First open its Settings and configure the view to show magnitude 4.5 and larger events worldwide over the past 7 days, and tell me the exact filter the app now reports in its heading along with how many earthquakes match. Then sort the list so the strongest event is on top, and record that top event's magnitude, its place description, and its origin time exactly as shown including the time-zone label the app is using. Open that top event's own page and pull its focal details - the depth and the latitude/longitude coordinates - and also check its Regional Information for the nearest populated place and that town's population, plus note whether the event has any "Did You Feel It?" felt reports. Next widen the time window in Settings to the past 30 days (same magnitude 4.5+ worldwide filter) and tell me whether the top event changes, and if it does, what the new leader's magnitude and place are. As a cross-check, switch the sort to newest-first on that 30-day list and tell me how recent the most recent qualifying event is. Finally, using everything you gathered, tell me which single earthquake I should lead the briefing with as the most significant recent event, and justify it on the specific figures - magnitude, depth, region, and whether it was felt or near population - rather than magnitude alone.

R1.requirement: In the USGS Latest Earthquakes app, use Settings to apply a magnitude 4.5+, worldwide, past-7-days view, then report the filter the app states in its list heading and the number of matching earthquakes.
R1.verification: Verified if the answer reports the applied filter as shown in the list header (magnitude 4.5+ over the past week / 7 days) and gives the result count the app prints as 'N earthquakes' for that view; the header text (e.g. 'USGS Magnitude 4.5+ Earthquakes, Past Week') and count line must be consistent with a live 4.5+/past-week worldwide setting (count value is whatever the live query returns).

R2.requirement: With that 7-day 4.5+ view, set the Sort control to largest-magnitude-first and record the top event's magnitude, place description, and origin time including the time-zone label shown.
R2.verification: Verified if the answer identifies the #1 event under Largest-Magnitude-First sort and reports its magnitude, its place string (e.g. a 'NN km <direction> of <place>, <country>' string or a region name), and the origin time copied with the app's time-zone label (the user tz such as 'UTC-07:00' or 'UTC'); must be the top row of the sorted 7-day list, not an arbitrary event.

R3.requirement: Open that top event's own event page and record its depth and its latitude/longitude coordinates.
R3.verification: Verified if the answer gives the depth (km) and the lat/lon coordinates from the event page for the same event named in R2; values must match that event's detail page, not the list summary alone.

R4.requirement: On the same event page, check Regional Information for the nearest populated place (name and population) and state whether the event has any 'Did You Feel It?' felt reports.
R4.verification: Verified if the answer reports, for the R2/R3 event, the nearest 'Nearby Places' entry with its population figure (or explicitly states Regional Information lists no populated place for a remote/ocean event), and states whether a 'Did You Feel It?' felt report exists (present vs none); grounded in the event page's Regional Information and Impact/DYFI sections.

R5.requirement: Widen the window in Settings to the past 30 days (same 4.5+ worldwide filter) and report whether the top event changes; if it changes, give the new leader's magnitude and place. Then switch Sort to newest-first and report how recent the most recent qualifying event is.
R5.verification: Verified if the answer states whether the largest-magnitude event over 30 days is the same as in R2 or different (giving the new leader's magnitude and place when it differs), and after re-sorting newest-first on the 30-day list reports the origin time (with tz label) of the most recent 4.5+ event; both reflect the live 30-day worldwide view.

R6.requirement: Using the collected fields, name the single most significant recent earthquake to lead the briefing with and justify it on magnitude, depth, region, and felt/population impact rather than magnitude alone.
R6.verification: Verified if the answer selects one specific event (identified by magnitude + place) and justifies it by explicitly citing the recorded fields from R2-R5 - magnitude, depth, region, and whether it was felt or near a populated place - so the pick is defended on combined significance, not magnitude alone; the chosen event and cited figures must be consistent with the values reported earlier.

query: I want the most-cited recent work on retrieval-augmented generation. On Semantic Scholar, search it, filter to the last 3 years and computer science, sort by most citations, and give me the top paper - title, authors, year, venue, citation count, and whether there's a PDF or DOI. Then tell me what's newest if I sort by most recent instead.

R1.requirement: On Semantic Scholar, search 'retrieval augmented generation' and apply the Date Range (last ~3 years) and Fields of Study = Computer Science facets, then set 'Sort by Citation Count'; record the active facets and the sort label (or URL) plus the result count.
R1.verification: Grader reproduces the facets and Citation-Count sort on semanticscholar.org and matches the recorded state and count.

R2.requirement: Open the top paper and record title, first authors, year, venue, and citation count.
R2.verification: Grader opens the paper and matches the fields.

R3.requirement: Record whether Semantic Scholar shows a PDF link and whether a DOI link is shown or not observed for the top most-cited paper.
R3.verification: Grader confirms the PDF/DOI link and abstract element.

R4.requirement: Change the sort to Most Recent and record whether the top paper changes and whether the filters stayed applied.
R4.verification: Grader confirms the re-sort evidence and filter persistence.

query: I want to see how the 'Large language model' Wikipedia article has evolved. Open its revision history and tell me the most recent edit date, then compare a revision from around January 2023 with the current version and tell me two concrete things that changed. If there's no January 2023 revision, use the nearest one and say so.

R1.requirement: Open the English Wikipedia article 'Large language model', go to View history, and record the approximate revision count if shown and the most recent edit date.
R1.verification: Grader opens the View history page and matches the count/latest date.

R2.requirement: Navigate to a revision from around January 2023 (or nearest available) and open it, recording that revision's timestamp.
R2.verification: Grader opens the recorded old revision and matches the timestamp.

R3.requirement: Use the compare/diff feature against the current version and record two concrete differences between the ~January-2023 revision and the current version. Recording the diff URL/oldid is helpful but optional.
R3.verification: Grader confirms the two concrete differences by opening the compare/diff view (the diff URL/oldid is helpful but not required if the two differences are correct and checkable).

R4.requirement: If the target date has no revision, use the nearest one and say so.
R4.verification: Answer states the nearest-date handling where relevant.

query: I'm looking for Apollo 11 footage. On the Internet Archive, search 'Apollo 11', narrow it to movies, and give me the top item - title, uploader, date, and a downloadable format - plus whether it plays without an account. Then tell me what comes up top if I sort by date instead.

R1.requirement: On archive.org, search 'Apollo 11' and restrict Media Type to movies in the left facets; record the active movies facet (from the selected facet or the URL) and the current top item under whatever sort is active (the default relevance sort is fine).
R1.verification: Grader reproduces the search and movies facet on archive.org and matches the recorded facet and the current top item, without requiring a specific initial sort.

R2.requirement: Open the top item and record title, uploader/creator, and date.
R2.verification: Grader opens the item and matches the title, uploader/creator, and date.

R3.requirement: Record one downloadable format/file and whether the item plays or previews on the page.
R3.verification: Grader confirms the format and that the item is viewable on the page.

R4.requirement: Switch 'Sort by' to Date and record whether the top item changes and, if it does, the new top item's title.
R4.verification: Grader confirms the Date re-sort evidence and the recorded top-change (new top title when it changes).

query: I need a Python HTTP client library. On PyPI, search 'http client', filter to a Python 3 classifier and a relevant topic, and give me the top project - name, latest version and release date, license, and required Python version. Follow its homepage or source link to the repo, and tell me whether the top result changes when I drop one filter.

R1.requirement: On PyPI, search 'http client' and apply a 'Programming Language :: Python :: 3' classifier plus one Topic/Intended-Audience classifier from 'Filter by classifier', and set 'Order by'; record the active classifiers from the selected chips or the URL (c=...).
R1.verification: Grader reproduces the classifier filters and ordering on pypi.org and matches the recorded state.

R2.requirement: Open the top project and record name, latest version and release date, license, and Requires Python if shown.
R2.verification: Grader opens the project page and matches the fields.

R3.requirement: Follow the Homepage or Source repository link and record the repository name, or write not shown.
R3.verification: Grader opens the linked repository or confirms the not-shown note.

R4.requirement: Remove or change one classifier filter and record whether the top result changes.
R4.verification: Grader confirms the changed-filter evidence.

query: 我想系统入门人工智能，在学堂在线上比较几门课后选一门报名。先搜索“人工智能”，用页面顶部的 Subject（学科）/Type（类型）或开课院校筛选缩小范围，记下生效证据（选中的筛选项、地址栏参数或“X courses found”计数）和结果列表；把排在前面的三门课分别记下来——课程名、主讲老师、开课院校、选课人数、课程简介里的方向一句话；然后换一个筛选（比如换个学科或院校），记下排在最前的课有没有变、其他筛选是否保留；再对比这三门课的选课人数、开课院校和内容方向，判断哪门更适合零基础、想要完整知识体系的人；最后给我结论：选哪门报名、为什么，它比落选的两门强在哪（理由要落在选课人数、院校或方向这些你记下的字段上）。

R1.requirement: 在学堂在线搜索“人工智能”，用页面顶部的 Subject(学科)/Type(类型)或开课院校筛选缩小范围，记录生效证据(选中的筛选项或地址栏参数)及结果列表。
R1.verification: 评分者按记录的筛选在学堂在线复现并核对结果列表。

R2.requirement: 记录排在最前一门课的课程名、主讲老师、开课院校、选课人数及课程简介里的方向一句话。
R2.verification: 评分者在搜索结果页核对该课的上述字段。

R3.requirement: 再记录第二、第三门课的课程名、主讲老师、开课院校、选课人数，以及各自课程简介里的方向一句话，作为候选。
R3.verification: 评分者在结果页/课程详情核对这两门课的课程名、主讲老师、开课院校、选课人数与方向描述。

R4.requirement: 换一个筛选(如换学科或院校)，记录排在最前的课是否变化、其它筛选是否保留。
R4.verification: 评分者复现换筛选并核对靠前课程是否变化。

R5.requirement: 对比这三门课的选课人数、开课院校与内容方向，判断哪门更适合零基础、想要完整知识体系的人。
R5.verification: 评分者确认对比基于 R2/R3 记录的具体字段。

R6.requirement: 给出结论：选哪门报名、为什么，它比落选的两门强在哪(理由须落在选课人数、院校或方向等已记录字段上)。
R6.verification: 评分者确认结论片在所引字段上确实占优。

query: 我想在起点找本正在连载、人气高的玄幻小说追。进玄幻分类，筛连载状态、按人气排序，挑字数别太少的（最好几百万字以上），把排前面的一本给我——书名、作者、字数、连载状态、最新章节和更新时间，再对比一两本说说为什么选它。最后按更新时间排一下，看排第一的会不会变。

R1.requirement: 进入起点中文网的“玄幻”分类页，把状态筛为“连载”、排序切为“人气”，记录生效证据(选中的玄幻分类与连载状态、当前“人气”排序值或地址栏参数)。
R1.verification: 评分者按记录证据在 qidian.com 复现：确认是玄幻分类、连载状态、且排序为人气(非字数或其它)。

R2.requirement: 打开排名靠前作品详情页，记录书名、作者、字数、连载状态、最新章节标题及更新时间。
R2.verification: 评分者打开作品详情页核对字段。

R3.requirement: 把排序切为“更新”，记录排名第一是否变化、筛选是否保留。
R3.verification: 评分者确认重排证据与筛选保留。

R4.requirement: 把榜首作品和列表上另外一两本对比(用字数、连载状态、人气/月票、最新章节更新时间这些具体字段)，说明为什么这本最值得追，而不是只报一个榜首。
R4.verification: 答案给出基于具体字段的择优比较，明确指出选它而非其它几本的理由，而非泛泛而谈。

query: 帮我在博客园某个技术标签（比如 Python 或前端）下找一篇热门好文。按推荐或热门排序（能按时间筛就筛近一周），把靠前的一篇给我——标题、作者、阅读量或推荐数、发布时间，并摘出正文里一个具体的 API 或语法说法。再到 Python 官方文档或 MDN 对一下这个说法对不对。顺便看看这篇热门文章是不是就是该分类默认最新列表里排在最前的那篇。

R1.requirement: 进入博客园某技术分类页(如 cnblogs.com/cate/python/)，查看“编辑推荐/10天推荐排行/15天热门博文”等排行入口，记录生效证据(选中的分类或排行标签、地址栏路径)及可见列表。
R1.verification: 评分者按记录证据在 cnblogs.com 复现分类与排行入口。

R2.requirement: 打开排名靠前文章，记录标题、作者、阅读量或推荐数、发布时间，并从正文摘录一个具体 API 或语法说法。
R2.verification: 评分者打开文章核对元数据与所摘说法。

R3.requirement: 打开对应官方文档(Python 官方文档或 MDN)核对该说法是否一致，无法证实要写明。
R3.verification: 评分者打开官方文档核对一致性。

R4.requirement: 对比 R1 排行入口（编辑推荐/热门博文）里靠前的文章与该分类页默认列表（按时间的最新随笔）里排在最前的文章，记录两者是否为同一篇。
R4.verification: 评分者在 cnblogs.com/cate/python/ 确认排行入口榜首与分类页最新列表榜首的对比证据。

query: 周末想按“最近大家都在看什么”在B站找几个视频，帮我跨分区看看各区的爆款，最后定今晚先看哪个知识类的。先打开B站排行榜，告诉我默认这个榜是按什么排的（榜单页写的规则）以及顶部都能切换到哪些分区；把默认“全部”榜前三名给我——标题、UP主、播放量；再切到“知识”分区，记下能证明切过去的证据（高亮的分区标签或地址栏），以及这个区前三名的标题、UP主、播放量或综合得分；然后再切到“科技数码”或“游戏”分区，同样记前三名；对比一下这几个区第一名的播放量量级和内容类型，指出哪个区整体更火、或有没有哪类题材跨区都上榜；最后从“知识”区里给我推荐今晚先看的一个，说明为什么选它（基于播放量、得分、UP主或标题主题），它比同区其他几个强在哪。

R1.requirement: 进入哔哩哔哩排行榜，记录默认“全部”榜的排序说明(榜单页写的规则)及顶部可切换的分区标签。
R1.verification: 评分者打开B站排行榜核对排序说明与分区标签。

R2.requirement: 记录默认“全部”榜前三名的标题、UP主、播放量。
R2.verification: 评分者在全部榜核对前三名字段。

R3.requirement: 切换到“知识”分区，记录能证明已切换的证据(高亮分区标签或地址栏参数)及该区前三名的标题、UP主、播放量或综合得分。
R3.verification: 评分者切到知识区核对切换证据与前三名。

R4.requirement: 再切换到“科技数码”或“游戏”分区，记录该区前三名的标题、UP主、播放量。
R4.verification: 评分者切到该区核对前三名。

R5.requirement: 对比这几个区第一名的播放量量级与内容类型，指出哪个区整体更火或有无题材跨区都上榜。
R5.verification: 评分者确认对比基于 R2-R4 记录的字段。

R6.requirement: 从“知识”区推荐今晚先看的一个，说明为什么选它(基于播放量、得分、UP主或标题主题)，它比同区其他几个强在哪。
R6.verification: 评分者确认推荐片在所引榜单字段上占优。

query: 我每周五要在团队群里发一段本周前端动态的小速报。帮我在掘金上整理这期的料：进前端相关的频道或标签页，按热门看排前面的三篇文章，把每篇的标题、作者和点赞数记下来；然后逐篇点进正文，记录发布时间和开头一两句讲的重点，顺便判断这篇属于哪类——框架或工具更新、浏览器新特性、还是经验教程；最后从这三篇里选一篇当速报头条——优先选发布时间最近、且属于框架更新或浏览器新特性的那篇，要是最新的一篇不属于这两类，就说明你为什么换成另一篇；把三篇按发布时间从新到旧排好，给我一段两三句话的速报草稿。

R1.requirement: 在掘金前端频道或标签页按热门记录排前三篇文章的标题、作者与点赞数。
R1.verification: 评分者打开该列表核对三篇条目。

R2.requirement: 逐篇打开正文，记录每篇的发布时间与开头要点，并为每篇标注类别（框架/工具更新、浏览器新特性、经验教程）。
R2.verification: 评分者打开三篇文章核对发布时间、要点与类别标注。

R3.requirement: 按发布时间从新到旧排列三篇，并选定头条：优先最新且属于框架更新或浏览器新特性；若最新一篇不属该类，须说明改选依据。
R3.verification: 评分者按所记时间核对排序正确且头条选择符合规则或给出了改选依据。

R4.requirement: 产出两三句话的速报草稿，涵盖头条要点与另两篇的一句概括。
R4.verification: 评分者确认草稿内容与 R1/R2 所记字段一致。

query: 我是后端方向的开发者，在找纯远程的全职工作，人在东八区，最在意时区限制合不合适、薪资写没写清楚。请上 Remote OK 帮我圈一圈：进后端相关的职位列表，收集四五条最近的招聘——职位名、公司、列表上带的标签（比如地区限制、薪资范围、全职与否）和发布时间；再挑其中两条点进详情，逐条记录工作地点或时区地区政策（比如仅限美国、或要求和某时区重叠几小时）、正文里一条关键岗位要求、以及薪资写明了没有（写了记范围，没写就注明）；然后按岗位对口、时区跟东八区兼容、薪资透明这三条比较这两条，明确说优先投哪份、为什么；最后提醒我选中这份还有什么要再确认的，比如时区重叠的具体要求或者薪资没写明。

R1.requirement: 在 Remote OK 的后端职位列表收集4到5条招聘：职位名、公司、列表标签（地区/薪资/全职）与发布时间。
R1.verification: 评分者打开该列表核对所记条目与标签。

R2.requirement: 打开其中两条职位详情页，各记录工作地点或时区地区政策、一条关键岗位要求、以及薪资是否写明（写明则记录范围），三项须来自对应职位自己的详情页。
R2.verification: 评分者打开两条详情页核对三项，确认未跨条拼凑。

R3.requirement: 按方向对口、与东八区的时区兼容性、薪资透明三条比较两条职位，明确选出优先投的一份，理由落在所记的政策与薪资字段上。
R3.verification: 评分者确认结论指向唯一一份且理由与 R2 所记字段一致。

R4.requirement: 指出选中职位需再核实的一项（如时区重叠的具体小时数要求，或薪资未写明）。
R4.verification: 评分者确认该提醒对应真实记录的字段。

query: 我想系统入门“机器学习”，打算用百度百科顺着相关词条给自己理一张概念地图。先在百度百科打开“机器学习”词条，把首段定义抄给我，并记下它的义项名（标题括号里那个）和词条 URL 里的数字 ID；再看词条顶部的“目录”，告诉我它把机器学习分成了哪几大块（比如主要流派、基本原理、相关法规、研究现状之类），任选一块记下这块下面的两个子标题；然后从页面里的相关词条或相关星图点进一个直接相关的词条（比如深度学习或人工智能），抄它的首段定义并说明它和机器学习是什么关系；接着再从这第二个词条继续点进它页面里的一个相关词条（第三跳），记下词条名和一句关键信息，形成“机器学习→X→Y”的浏览链；再回到机器学习词条，在“历史发展”或“相关法规”那块里找一条带年份或国家的具体事实记下来。最后用收集到的内容给我两三句话的入门小结：机器学习是什么、两个关键相关概念、以及一条发展或法规事实。

R1.requirement: 打开百度百科“机器学习”词条，抄录首段定义，记录义项名(标题括号内，如“多领域交叉学科”)及 URL 中的词条数字 ID(lemmaId，如 217599)。
R1.verification: 评分者打开该词条核对首段定义、义项名与词条 ID。

R2.requirement: 从词条顶部“目录”读出机器学习被分成的主要板块(如主要流派/基本原理/相关法规/研究现状)，列出其中至少三块，并任选一块记录其下两个子标题。
R2.verification: 评分者在词条目录核对所列板块与两个子标题。

R3.requirement: 从页面的相关词条或相关星图点进一个直接相关词条(如深度学习或人工智能)，抄其首段定义，并说明它与机器学习的关系。
R3.verification: 评分者打开该相关词条核对定义并确认关系说明成立(依赖 R1)。

R4.requirement: 从 R3 的词条再点进它页面内的一个相关词条(第三跳)，记录词条名与一句关键信息，形成“机器学习→X→Y”的三跳浏览链。
R4.verification: 评分者沿链核对第三跳词条确由第二个词条页面可达且信息属实。

R5.requirement: 回到机器学习词条，在“历史发展”或“相关法规”板块记录一条带年份或国家的具体事实。
R5.verification: 评分者在对应板块核对该事实。

R6.requirement: 综合前五步给出两三句入门小结：机器学习是什么、两个关键相关概念(来自 R3/R4)、以及一条发展或法规事实(来自 R5)。
R6.verification: 答案小结覆盖三要素且与所记字段一致。

query: I'm building an internal shortlist of open-weight LLMs and want to triangulate model-card scores against an independent leaderboard and, for the largest apparent discrepancy, against the original technical report. Start with three models on Hugging Face: DeepSeek-V3, Microsoft phi-4, and DeepSeek V4 Pro. From each model card's results table, pull its self-reported MMLU-Pro (or MMLU), HumanEval, and MATH scores, noting when a metric is absent or uses a different variant. Next find each model on Artificial Analysis and read its Intelligence Index plus one named component if displayed. Because these metrics are not directly equivalent, compare only their broad direction and limitations, then identify one model whose self-report is best corroborated and one with the largest discrepancy. For the latter, open its technical report on arXiv and check whether the report supports or qualifies the relevant card claim. Finally give one shortlist recommendation and explicitly reconcile disagreements among the sources.

R1.requirement: From the evaluation table on the DeepSeek-V3 Hugging Face card, record its self-reported MMLU-Pro (EM), HumanEval-Mul (Pass@1), and MATH-500 (EM) scores.
R1.verification: Three named scores are reported for DeepSeek-V3 exactly as displayed on the selected Hugging Face card table, with metric variants and units attributed.

R2.requirement: From the evaluation table on the phi-4 Hugging Face card, record its self-reported MMLU, HumanEval, and MATH scores.
R2.verification: Three named scores are reported for phi-4 exactly as displayed on its Hugging Face card, with metric variants and units attributed.

R3.requirement: From the evaluation table on the DeepSeek V4 Pro Hugging Face card, record its self-reported MMLU-Pro (or MMLU), HumanEval, and MATH scores, with clear table/variant attribution; if a chosen variant table lacks one of these metrics, explicitly state that it is not shown for that variant rather than mixing values across variants without labeling.
R3.verification: MMLU/MMLU-Pro, HumanEval, and MATH are each reported when displayed for the chosen DeepSeek V4 Pro card/table variant; absent metrics are explicitly marked absent and values are not mixed across unlabeled variants.

R4.requirement: On Artificial Analysis, locate all three models and record each one's Intelligence Index plus at least one named component/sub-benchmark when the page displays one; if a per-model component value is not shown, record the component family only and clearly state that no numeric component value was visible.
R4.verification: Judge confirms the Intelligence Index value plus at least one named index component is recorded for the models (component family name suffices).

R5.requirement: Name exactly one model as best-corroborated and exactly one as having the largest apparent discrepancy, each justified by the recorded card and Artificial Analysis evidence while explicitly noting that the benchmark families and aggregate index are not directly interchangeable.
R5.verification: Exactly one model is named best-corroborated and exactly one as having the largest apparent discrepancy. The decision is accepted if it follows from the recorded evidence and acknowledges metric non-equivalence; no particular model is preselected as the expected winner or loser.

R6.requirement: For the model identified in R5 as having the largest apparent discrepancy, open its own technical report on arXiv when one is available and state the report's relevant strength or benchmark claim; if no report can be located, record that limitation rather than substituting another model.
R6.verification: Judge confirms the agent stated the report's central strength claim (e.g. phi-4 outscoring GPT-4o on GPQA/MATH), whether sourced from the abstract or the body.

R7.requirement: Judge whether the report supports, qualifies, or does not address the relevant card claim, then explain how that finding relates to—but is not directly equivalent to—the independent Intelligence Index.
R7.verification: An explicit statement reconciles the card headline number against the arXiv report's framing and the low Intelligence Index, noting whether the report's qualitative claim is consistent with the independent ranking or looks over-stated.

R8.requirement: Give one shortlist decision weighing all three sources. The technical report needs to be used only for the model selected for report verification; do not imply that every recommended model was independently checked in its own report.
R8.verification: Exactly one model is recommended for the shortlist, justified by jointly citing its Hugging Face card scores, its Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and component sub-metric, and (for the most-inflated model) the arXiv report, with any card-vs-leaderboard-vs-report divergence explicitly reconciled.

query: Before I cite DeepSeek-V3 in a report, I want to be sure its Hugging Face summary is actually faithful to the original technical paper and isn't quietly dropping or softening numbers, and I want a neutral third opinion on whether the model is even worth citing for a coding-heavy use. Please read the DeepSeek-V3 technical report on arXiv: capture the headline claim from the abstract, and pull two concrete benchmark numbers that the paper reports for the model (for instance its MMLU-Pro and GPQA-Diamond figures). Then open the DeepSeek-V3 model card on Hugging Face and check, benchmark by benchmark, whether those same two numbers appear there and whether they match, differ, or are missing. Next go to Artificial Analysis, find DeepSeek-V3, and read its independent Intelligence Index and one component score so I have an outside reference that owes nothing to the paper or the card. Finally give me a single trust verdict for a downstream coding assistant: is the card a faithful reflection of the report, and does the independent index support leaning on this model, calling out any place the three sources disagree.

R1.requirement: Open the DeepSeek-V3 technical report on arxiv.org (abs/2412.19437) and state the headline performance claim from its abstract.
R1.verification: The abstract's central claim is captured (that DeepSeek-V3 outperforms other open-source models and is comparable to leading closed-source models), attributed to the arXiv abstract.

R2.requirement: From the report body (introduction or a results table, not the abstract), record two specific named benchmarks and their numeric values reported for DeepSeek-V3.
R2.verification: Two benchmark names plus numeric scores are reported from the arXiv paper text (for example MMLU-Pro around 75.9 and GPQA around 59.1), identified as coming from the paper rather than the abstract.

R3.requirement: Open the DeepSeek-V3 Hugging Face card and determine, for each of the two benchmarks from R2, whether it appears in the card's own evaluation table.
R3.verification: For each of the two benchmarks a clear present/absent determination is given from the Hugging Face card, with the card's value quoted where present.

R4.requirement: Place each benchmark's report value beside its card value and state whether they match, differ, or are absent.
R4.verification: A benchmark-by-benchmark side-by-side is shown with the numbers, and each pair is labelled match/differ/absent (expected: MMLU-Pro 75.9 and GPQA 59.1 match between report and card).

R5.requirement: On Artificial Analysis, find DeepSeek-V3 and record its independent Intelligence Index plus one named component sub-metric of that index.
R5.verification: An Intelligence Index value (around 14) and one named component (for example GPQA Diamond or Humanity's Last Exam) are reported for DeepSeek-V3 from Artificial Analysis.

R6.requirement: State whether the independent Artificial Analysis standing is broadly consistent with the strong self-reported numbers on the card and in the report, or whether it tempers them.
R6.verification: An explicit consistency judgement is given comparing the card/report numbers against the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and component, noting agreement or tension with the numbers cited.

R7.requirement: Give a single faithfulness verdict on whether the Hugging Face card faithfully reflects the arXiv report, explicitly naming any benchmark omitted from the card or whose number diverges.
R7.verification: One faithfulness verdict is stated, grounded in the R4 comparison, explicitly naming any omitted or divergent benchmark or stating none was found.

R8.requirement: Give a final trust decision for a downstream coding assistant, reconciling the report, the card, and the independent index.
R8.verification: A single trust recommendation (cite / cite-with-caveat / do-not-rely) is given, justified by jointly citing the arXiv numbers, the card's fidelity from R7, and the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and component, and it explicitly addresses any three-way disagreement.

query: My startup is building a closed-source commercial coding assistant and we want to fine-tune an open code model, but legal is nervous about licenses. I'm weighing WizardCoder-15B-V1.0, Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct, and Microsoft phi-4. First, gather each candidate's license from its Hugging Face card and a coding-relevant benchmark where available. Then check each license on Choose a License when that site covers it, and independently read the LICENSE text in the model's own repository. For each candidate, state whether the displayed terms appear to permit use in a proprietary commercial product and list continuing obligations or restrictions; if a license is not covered by Choose a License, say so and rely on the actual license text rather than treating absence as automatic rejection. Among candidates whose terms can be confirmed, use Artificial Analysis as a capability tiebreaker. Recommend exactly one model, flag tensions between card benchmarks and the independent index, and present the licensing conclusion as an engineering screen rather than legal advice.

R1.requirement: Read the license tag on each candidate's Hugging Face card: WizardCoder-15B-V1.0, Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct, and phi-4.
R1.verification: A license is reported for each model from its Hugging Face card: bigscience-openrail-m (OpenRAIL-M) for WizardCoder-15B-V1.0, apache-2.0 for Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct, and mit for phi-4.

R2.requirement: From each card, record a coding-relevant benchmark where the card reports one (at minimum WizardCoder-15B-V1.0's HumanEval and phi-4's HumanEval), and note where a card provides no benchmark table.
R2.verification: WizardCoder's HumanEval (around 59.8) and phi-4's HumanEval (around 82.6) are recorded from their cards, and it is noted that the Qwen2.5-Coder card shows no benchmark table (points to an external blog).

R3.requirement: On choosealicense.com, check the licenses it actually catalogs and record their displayed commercial-use permission, conditions, and limitations. If OpenRAIL-M or another license is not covered, explicitly record that absence without treating it alone as proof that commercial closed-source use is forbidden.
R3.verification: Apache-2.0 and MIT are each confirmed on choosealicense.com to permit commercial use with no copyleft; it is noted that OpenRAIL-M is a Responsible-AI use-restricted license not covered among choosealicense.com's standard licenses and so cannot be confirmed there as permitting unrestricted commercial closed-source use.

R4.requirement: For each candidate, read the LICENSE file or authoritative license text in the model's own repository and compare it with the Hugging Face tag and any Choose a License entry, recording relevant notice, attribution, patent, use-restriction, source-disclosure, or redistribution obligations.
R4.verification: Judge confirms the agent read the finally-recommended model's own GitHub LICENSE and reported it (e.g. MIT/Apache-2.0); a rejected candidate's repo need NOT be confirmed.

R5.requirement: Classify each candidate conditionally from the collected license text: confirmed compatible with the stated proprietary-product use, incompatible, or unresolved. Do not describe MIT, Apache-2.0, or another permissive license as 'unrestricted'; list the obligations that remain.
R5.verification: WizardCoder-15B-V1.0 (OpenRAIL-M) is explicitly excluded on the licensing ground from R3, leaving Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct and phi-4 as the confirmed-permissive survivors.

R6.requirement: For each surviving candidate, read its Intelligence Index on Artificial Analysis as an independent capability tiebreaker.
R6.verification: An Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index is reported for each survivor (phi-4 around 5; and the Qwen model's index if listed, or an explicit note that it is not indexed on Artificial Analysis), attributed to Artificial Analysis.

R7.requirement: Recommend exactly one model among candidates whose license status is sufficiently confirmed, justified by the recorded license obligations and independent capability evidence. State that this is a benchmark engineering screen rather than a substitute for legal review.
R7.verification: One model is recommended and justified jointly by its confirmed commercially-permissive license (card + repo + reference) and its Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index versus the other survivor, not on card benchmarks alone.

R8.requirement: Flag any place where a model's self-reported card benchmark and its independent Artificial Analysis index point in different directions.
R8.verification: At least one concrete card-vs-independent tension is named with numbers (for example phi-4's high self-reported HumanEval around 82.6 against its low Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index around 5), or it is explicitly stated that the surviving models could not both be cross-checked because one is not indexed.

query: We're picking a model to power a production customer-support chatbot and I keep hearing about the newest 2026 open-weight releases. I want to decide whether to adopt one now or wait, and I refuse to trust a single vendor's numbers. Please go to Artificial Analysis, pick one of the newest 2026-released open-weight models sitting near the top of the Intelligence Index (for example GLM-5.2), and record its Intelligence Index, its blended price per million tokens, and its output speed. Then cross-verify its real hosting economics across three independent hosting sources: read its cheapest input and output price plus context window on OpenRouter, read its input, cached-input, and output price plus context window on Together AI, and check whether Replicate lists it for per-token inference at all. Also open the model's Hugging Face card to confirm its license and native context length. R6.verification: The selected model's license and native context length are reported from its Hugging Face card as displayed, without assuming a particular model.

R7.verification: At least one concrete cross-source discrepancy is named with numbers (for example Together listing a smaller context or higher price than OpenRouter, and Replicate not carrying the model), grounded in the recorded figures.

R8.verification: A single adopt-or-wait decision is stated and justified by jointly citing the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and speed, the OpenRouter and Together prices and context windows, the Replicate availability finding, and the card's license, with the reconciliation from R7 feeding the call.

query: I'm choosing a model for a high-volume document summarization feature where cost matters but quality still matters, and I won't rank on a single price source. R4.requirement: On OpenRouter's own model pages (openrouter.ai), record each model's cheapest input and output price per 1M tokens as a second independent commercial source; the figures must be read from OpenRouter's own pages, not from a search-engine snippet.

R5.requirement: For each provider separately, calculate the cost of 5M input tokens plus 1M output tokens for each model, then rank the models by Intelligence Index divided by that aligned workload cost.

query: I want a focused reading path of three to four papers on LLM agents for tabular-data analysis and text-to-SQL, where each paper is picked because it fills a gap the previous one exposes, and I care above all about whether I could actually reproduce each one. For every paper I need the title, first author, and year, plus a real check of its runnable artifacts: is the code public on GitHub and under what license, and does a matching dataset or model live on Hugging Face. Finally put the papers in reading order, explain how each sets up the next, and make a defensible call on which single paper has the most reproducible artifacts using evidence from more than one source.

R1.requirement: 记录第一篇论文（由你从 LLM agent 用于 tabular-data 分析 / text-to-SQL 方向自选），含标题、第一作者与年份，并说明它针对的、相对既有工作的具体空白。
R1.verification: A first on-topic paper is recorded with title, first author, year, and the specific gap it addresses. Any relevant paper is acceptable; no fixed arXiv ID, benchmark, scale, or numeric result is required.

R2.requirement: 记录第二篇论文，含标题、第一作者与年份，并说明它相对第一篇打开的新空白（第一篇暴露出的某个能力或评测缺口）。
R2.verification: A distinct second on-topic paper is recorded with title, first author, year, and a clear explanation of the new gap it addresses relative to the first. No fixed paper is required.

R3.requirement: 记录第三篇论文，含标题/作者/年份，并说明它点出的、前两篇仍未覆盖的空白；三篇须互不相同且均切题。
R3.verification: A distinct third on-topic paper is recorded with title, first author, year, and the gap left by the first two that it addresses. No fixed paper is required.

R4.requirement: 记录第四篇论文（可选）：若纳入，则选它填补前三篇留下的空白（如交互式/agentic text-to-SQL 或 tabular-agent 方向），含标题、年份与该空白。阅读路径至少三篇互不相同且切题即可，不强制满四篇。
R4.verification: 评分者确认阅读路径含≥3篇互不相同、切题的论文；若纳入第四篇则其填补前三篇空白。不因少于四篇而判 fail。

R5.requirement: Checks each paper for public code on GitHub and records the repository AND its license.
R5.verification: For every selected paper, a matching GitHub repository is checked and its actual displayed license is recorded; a missing repository or missing license may be reported explicitly and counts as a reproducibility finding rather than forcing a particular paper set.

R6.requirement: Checks whether a matching dataset or model lives on Hugging Face for each paper and records the concrete finding, reconciling it against the GitHub finding rather than assuming.
R6.verification: For every selected paper, Hugging Face is checked for a matching dataset or model and the concrete finding is recorded, including an explicit 'not found' result when appropriate. No particular organization or dataset is preselected.

R7.requirement: 在你所选的论文（≥3篇）上构建有序阅读路径，逐一说明前一篇如何引出后一篇所回答的问题。
R7.verification: 评分者确认给出有序阅读路径并逐一说明承接关系；不锁定固定篇数。

R8.requirement: Delivers ONE synthesis decision: names the single paper with the MOST reproducible artifacts, justified by concrete evidence from at least two independent sources (GitHub repo+license AND a Hugging Face dataset/model), and contrasts it against a weaker-reproducibility paper in the set.
R8.verification: The final answer names exactly one 'most reproducible' paper and cites both its GitHub repo+license (R5) and its Hugging Face dataset/model (R6) as the two-source evidence, and explicitly contrasts it with a paper that has thinner artifacts (e.g. code-only, or data reused from another benchmark) so a single source could not have produced the verdict.

query: I run a weekly ML reading group and I want to choose this week's paper from what's currently trending on Hugging Face Daily Papers, but I refuse to pick on hype alone. query: I want the best small-but-capable open model to run locally, so I'm comparing capability against size and I don't want to trust one leaderboard blindly. From that I'll compute intelligence-per-parameter to find the best pick, cross-verify my winner across all three sources, and flag one clearly oversized model that underperforms for its size. My final pick and my flag both have to be grounded in the specific index scores, parameter counts, and report claims I read, including how I handle any place where the sources disagree on the parameter count.

R1.verification: On artificialanalysis.ai (the small open-source models 4B-40B view or each model's page) four named models are located and their Intelligence Index values are recorded, e.g. Qwen3.5 9B (Reasoning) 21, Gemma 4 12B (Reasoning) 22, Nemotron Cascade 2 30B A3B 21, and one further small model (e.g. Qwen3.6 27B Reasoning 37), each score read from the page.

R2.requirement: 从最小的高性价比候选（你所选）的非门控 Hugging Face 卡片读取其参数规模。
R2.verification: On huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B (publicly viewable, not gated) the card's stated parameter count (9B) is recorded, and it is noted whether this differs from the parameter figure Artificial Analysis lists for the same model.

R3.requirement: From the candidate flagged as potentially oversized, read the parameter figures from its non-gated Hugging Face card; for a mixture-of-experts model, record both total and active parameters when shown.
R3.verification: On huggingface.co/nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B (non-gated) the card's parameter statement is recorded (~30-32B total with 3B activated, an MoE), and the total-vs-active distinction is noted for use in the size comparison.

R4.requirement: 再从第三个模型的非门控 Hugging Face 卡片读取参数规模以拓宽对比，并确认其无需登录即可查看。
R4.verification: A third small/mid model's non-gated HF card is opened, its stated parameter size recorded, and the card confirmed viewable without login/gating, so its size can be set against its R1 Intelligence Index.

R5.requirement: 对你选出的赢家与被标记的偏大模型，各查其本身的 arXiv 或技术报告作为第三来源，记录其参数/能力主张。
R5.verification: The relevant technical report is opened, e.g. Nemotron-Cascade 2 at arXiv 2603.19220 (30B MoE, 3B activated; second open-weight model to reach 2025 IMO/IOI/ICPC Gold-level, ~20x fewer parameters), and its stated parameters/capability are recorded and compared against the HF card and Artificial Analysis figures for the same model.

R7.verification: At least one concrete discrepancy is surfaced and resolved, e.g. Nemotron Cascade 2 listed ~31.6B on Artificial Analysis vs ~30-32B total / 3B activated on the HF card and arXiv, or the Qwen3.5 9B vs 9.7B gap; the answer states which parameter figure it adopts (e.g. total vs active for an MoE) and why that choice is fair for a 'run locally' comparison.

R8.requirement: Name one best-ratio model and one model that appears oversized relative to its index under the declared parameter convention, grounding both in the recorded Artificial Analysis, Hugging Face, and report evidence. No specific model is preselected.
R8.verification: The final answer names one best intelligence-per-parameter model and one oversized underperformer, and the reasoning divides/compares the exact index numbers from R1 against the exact parameter counts from R2-R4 (with the R7 reconciliation and the R5 report claim), reconciling all three sources so a single page could not have produced the verdict; the flagged model is shown to carry more parameters yet match or trail a smaller model's index.

query: I'm choosing a database for a brand-new time-series analytics service and I want a genuinely data-driven pick, not marketing hype. I also want to sanity-check popularity against a second, independent signal rather than trusting a single ranking, and before I commit I want to know the chosen system's real support runway. R1.requirement: From DB-Engines' Time Series DBMS category ranking, record the top 5 systems in rank order, each with its Rank number and its current 'Score' value.

R3.verification: Agent names three shortlisted candidates (must include TimescaleDB) and cites the TigerData/Timescale docs stating hypertables are PostgreSQL tables that automatically partition time-series data by time and optionally by other dimensions.

R4.requirement: On Prometheus's official documentation, verify its differentiating claim that it is a pull-based metrics system with its own query language, PromQL.

R5.verification: Agent cites the third candidate's official docs for a concrete differentiating feature (e.g. milvus.io docs naming multiple index types such as FLAT, IVF_FLAT, HNSW, SCANN), on a docs site distinct from R3 and R4.

R6.requirement: Add a second, independent popularity signal by recording the GitHub star count of at least two of the three shortlisted systems' primary repositories, and state whether this second signal agrees or disagrees with the DB-Engines ordering for those systems.

R8.requirement: Cross-check the recommended system's own release/support documentation or endoflife.date entry. If only an underlying engine's support horizon is available, label it explicitly as the engine's runway and do not present it as the shortlisted product's support commitment.
R8.verification: Agent opens the recommended system's endoflife.date page (e.g. postgresql for a TimescaleDB pick, showing PostgreSQL 18 supported to ~14 Nov 2030) and states the support-end date, tying it back into the final recommendation as a runway check rather than leaving support unaddressed.
